title: Support ##[M:N] range cycle delay in SVA sequences
body:
## Summary
This patch adds support for `##[M:N]` range cycle delay expressions in SVA sequences (IEEE 1800-2023 16.7). A range delay `a ##[M:N] b` means `b` must hold at some clock tick between M and N cycles after `a`. The implementation linearizes the SExpr tree into sequence steps, then expands range delays into an if/delay check chain within a PExpr -- this handles both standalone and multi-step sequences (e.g., `##[1:2] b ##1 c`).

## Reproducer
```systemverilog
module t;
  logic clk = 0;
  always #5 clk = ~clk;
  int cyc = 0;
  always @(posedge clk) cyc <= cyc + 1;
  event e;
  always @(negedge clk) if (cyc >= 1 && cyc <= 10) ->e;
  assert property (@(e) ##[1:3] 1);
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: Unsupported: ## range cycle delay range expression
```

## Changes
- `V3AstNodeStmt.h`: Added `op3 := rhsp` to `AstDelay` for range max bound; added `isRangeDelay()` accessor
- `V3AstNodes.cpp`: Added `[RANGE]` to `AstDelay::dump()`; added `isRangeDelay` to `dumpJson()`
- `verilog.y`: Parse `##[M:N]` range into `AstDelay{min, max}` instead of emitting BBUNSUP
- `V3AssertProp.cpp`: Added `RangeDelayExpander` pass that linearizes SExpr trees into sequence steps and expands range delays into if/delay check chains within PExpr; runs before the DFA graph builder
- `V3EmitV.cpp`: Emit `##[M:N]` syntax for range delays in Verilog re-emission

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_property_sexpr_range_delay.{v,py}` -- functional test for `##[1:3]`, `##[2:4]`, degenerate `##[2:2]`, and multi-step `##[1:2] b ##1 c`
- Added `test_regress/t/t_property_sexpr_range_delay_bad.{v,py,out}` -- error tests for non-constant bounds, negative bounds, max < min, and ##0 in range
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_sequence_sexpr_unsup.out` -- removed `## range cycle delay` unsupported errors
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_property_sexpr_parse_unsup.out` -- removed `## range cycle delay` and property-local-variable unsupported errors (now supported)

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
